features.description: Create project scaffold and runtime configuration for a local Telegram Bot, including environment validation for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS, runtime_state.json creation, logs directory creation, and startup failure when ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS is empty outside NODE_ENV=test.
Implement command parsing and authorization for the documented command whitelist, including rejection of unauthorized chat IDs, unknown slash commands, and missing required arguments.

Implement repository whitelist and workspace state management, including /repos, /use <repo>, /pwd, atomic runtime_state.json writes, exact alias matching, path normalization, and no-workspace errors.
Implement workspace inspection commands /ls and /git so they run only in the selected workspace and return bounded Telegram responses with branch, short status, and five recent commits for /git.

Implement the task executor abstraction with shell-disabled process spawning, task ID generation, task metadata persistence, task status transitions, stdout and stderr log persistence, exit code recording, and secret redaction for Telegram responses.

Implement /ask <question> as a read-only Codex discussion task that uses the required prompt rules, runs in the selected workspace, enforces a 10 minute timeout, writes a full task log, and marks status from process exit code.

Implement agent-workflow readiness checks for /work, /continue, and /run-orch by requiring AGENTS.md, SPEC.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, init.sh, and orchestrator.py at the selected workspace root before spawning processes.

Implement /work <requirement> as a Codex delegation task that rejects concurrent active workflow tasks in the same workspace, uses the required long-running workflow prompt, does not directly parse or rewrite target feature state, and logs all output.

Implement /continue <instruction> as a Codex recovery task that rejects concurrent active workflow tasks in the same workspace, uses the required repository-state reconstruction prompt, and logs all output.

Implement /run-orch <rounds> with integer validation for 1 through 5, concurrent workflow-task rejection, shell-disabled spawning of python3 orchestrator.py --max-rounds <rounds>, and full task logging.

Implement task management commands /status, /logs <task_id>, and /stop <task_id>, including active and recent finished task display, last 120 log lines, log path confinement, unknown task rejection, and SIGTERM only for Bot-recorded running tasks.

Implement /help and contract verification for the command surface, including exact help output, exact command whitelist, absence of /run-feature and /eval-feature, required prompt text checks, and runtime state schema checks.

Create root README.md with the exact required top-level sections, documented command surface, required environment variables, repository workflow model, runtime state and logs explanation, local setup instructions, verification command ./init.sh, and current limitations.

Create docs/deployment.md with the exact required top-level sections, trusted local host deployment model, environment variables, repository whitelist guidance, start command npm start, long-running operation guidance, runtime artifact boundaries for runtime_state.json and logs/, verification command ./init.sh, operational checks, and failure handling.

Reorganize documentation files by creating docs/README.md as the documentation index, placing the archived original specification at docs/archive/original-spec.md, placing the agent workflow reference at docs/reference/agent-workflow.md, keeping docs/deployment.md at docs/deployment.md, and removing obsolete documentation path references.

Normalize verification scripts by extracting init.sh state validation into scripts/verify-state.py, updating init.sh to call python3 scripts/verify-state.py, creating scripts/README.md documenting verify-state.py and smoke.js, and adding package.json test:smoke with the exact command node scripts/smoke.js while preserving the existing smoke command.

Implement Telegram webhook HTTP transport with GET /healthz, POST /telegram/webhook, PORT-based server startup, Telegram update parsing, app handler dispatch, Telegram sendMessage replies, invalid JSON handling, non-POST rejection, and tests with fake HTTP requests and fake Telegram API calls.

Implement GCP webhook deployment support by adding package.json start and webhook:set scripts, creating scripts/set-telegram-webhook.js, validating TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL, calling Telegram setWebhook, documenting GCP deployment steps in docs/deployment.md, and testing webhook registration behavior with fake Telegram API calls.

Implement npm start with the exact command node src/index.js, add repository whitelist configuration through REPO_WHITELIST_JSON, validate whitelist JSON aliases and paths at startup, update README.md and docs/deployment.md with the start command and whitelist configuration, and add automated coverage for valid and invalid whitelist configuration.

Generalize webhook deployment documentation and requirements from GCP-specific deployment to public server and VPS deployment, including VPS, VM, container, Cloud Run, hosted Node.js runtime, HTTPS webhook URL, runtime-local repository paths, persistent runtime_state.json and logs storage, and npm start as the webhook-mode command.

Implement Telegram long polling transport with npm run start:polling using the exact command node src/polling.js, Telegram getUpdates polling, update offset persistence in runtime_state.json, dispatch of valid messages into createApp().handleMessage(...), Telegram sendMessage replies, no TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL requirement, and fake Telegram API tests.

Rename the Telegram orchestrator command from /run-orch <rounds> to BotFather-compatible /run_orch <rounds>, remove /run-orch from the supported command whitelist, preserve existing rounds validation and python3 orchestrator.py --max-rounds <rounds> execution behavior, update help output, tests, README.md, docs/deployment.md, progress.md, and SPEC.md references, and document run_orch in the BotFather command menu.

Fix polling runtime state persistence so advancing telegramUpdateOffset after a handled update reloads and preserves app handler state changes, including /use <repo> currentRepo and cwd changes plus task metadata writes, while still advancing offsets for valid update IDs and ignored non-message updates, with automated polling tests for workspace preservation and task metadata preservation.

Extract and persist a redacted finalResult for each completed Bot task from its process log, keep full raw logs on disk, change /logs <task_id> to return the stored final result instead of raw process log tails for finished tasks, return a not-available-yet status message for running or stopping tasks, avoid exposing raw exec/source/test/diff process output to Telegram, and add automated coverage for Codex logs with tool output before the final answer, running-task responses, and no-result fallback.

Automatically send a Telegram completion message with task ID, final status, and stored finalResult to the originating chat when a Bot-started /ask, /work, /continue, or /run_orch task finishes, record the originating chatId in task metadata, preserve immediate task-start responses and /logs retrieval, avoid sending raw process logs, tolerate completion send failures without changing task status, and add fake Telegram API coverage for polling-mode completion pushes and no-result fallback.

Enhance extractFinalResultFromLog so Codex output containing a final answer, a tokens used block with token count, and a duplicate final answer returns exactly one copy of the final answer, excludes tokens used and the token count from finalResult, preserves existing extraction behavior, and adds unit test coverage for this duplicated-output shape.

Add ask session runtime schema support by storing ask session bindings keyed by authorized Telegram chatId and selected repository alias, preserving existing runtime_state.json fields, extracting Codex session IDs from ask task output when available, persisting codexSessionId and repo alias on ask task metadata, validating the expanded state shape, and adding unit coverage for state normalization and session ID extraction.

Make plain /ask <message> session-aware so it starts a new Codex ask task when no current chatId plus repo binding exists, resumes the bound Codex session with codex exec resume <session_id> <message> when a binding exists, keeps shell execution disabled, records task chatId, repo alias, and codexSessionId metadata, and adds unit and harness coverage for both new-session and resume paths.

Implement /ask subcommands for ask session management: /ask new <message> forces a new Codex ask session and replaces the current binding after session discovery, /ask resume <session_id> <message> stores the binding and resumes that session, /ask resume --last <message> runs codex exec resume --last and stores the discovered session ID, /ask exit removes only the current chatId plus repo binding, /ask session reports the current binding or explicit no-session-selected response, and /ask -- <message> treats reserved subcommand words as a literal question, with parser, help, contract, unit, and harness coverage.

Enhance /status and /logs <task_id> so tasks with codexSessionId metadata display the Codex session ID alongside existing task status and final result information, without exposing raw process logs or changing task IDs, and add task-management tests for session ID display and no-session fallback.

Preserve /work, /continue, and /run_orch as repository workflow task commands while ask session support is added, keeping their current command argv, concurrency rules, repository-state recovery semantics, task metadata, and tests unchanged except for explicit regression coverage proving they do not use ask session bindings or Codex ask resume behavior.

Implement Telegram approval decisions for pending agent approval requests, including explicit /approve <request_id>, /reject <request_id>, and /always_allow <request_id> commands; reply-based decisions where replying yes/approve approves, no/reject rejects, and always/always allow/以后都允许 approves and stores an equivalent future allow rule; safe request ID validation; rejection of unknown, expired, already-resolved, or unauthorized requests; and automated unit, harness, and contract coverage for command parsing, help output, reply correlation, authorization, and non-reply words not triggering decisions.

Fix Codex ask session metadata extraction so /ask, /ask new, /ask resume <session_id> <message>, and /ask resume --last <message> use Codex CLI structured JSONL output or trusted pre-answer CLI metadata for codexSessionId; do not let assistant final answers, source code, tests, README text, SPEC text, command output, or log tail text override the real session ID; preserve raw task logs and finalResult behavior; keep task_id as a Bot-local task identifier; make resumed ask task-start responses include the Codex session ID and a resumed-mode indicator; and add unit and harness regression coverage for logs containing a real session id before assistant output plus a fake Codex session string inside the answer.

Fix finalResult extraction for Codex CLI JSONL task logs so successful /ask tasks using codex exec --json or codex exec --json resume store the last user-facing assistant or agent message as finalResult; ignore JSONL command_execution events, aggregated command output, raw source output, tests, diffs, and internal metadata; preserve existing plain-text answer-marker extraction, token usage cleanup, duplicate final answer cleanup, raw local logs, Telegram truncation, and secret redaction; keep no-result fallback only when no final answer candidate exists; and add unit and task executor regression coverage using real-shaped JSONL logs with item.completed agent_message text.

Replace the public read-only /ask command with a general-purpose /agent command that supports /agent <instruction>, /agent new <instruction>, /agent resume <session_id|--last> <instruction>, /agent exit, /agent session, and /agent -- <instruction>; reuse the existing Codex session binding model by authorized Telegram chatId and selected repository alias; remove /ask, /work, and /run_orch from the public command whitelist, help output, README command surface, deployment command documentation, BotFather command menu documentation, and contract tests so they return Unknown command; keep /continue, /status, /logs, /stop, workspace commands, and approval commands available; update prompts so /agent is not categorically read-only, requires following AGENTS.md for implementation requests, preserves repository files and git history as source of truth, and summarizes actions, changed files, verification, and remaining issues; preserve shell-disabled spawning, JSONL session metadata extraction, JSONL finalResult extraction, completion pushes, secret redaction, Telegram truncation, runtime state normalization for legacy ask state, and automated unit, harness, contract, documentation, and smoke coverage.

Implement basic Codex permission prompt bridging for Bot-started /agent and /continue tasks by detecting Codex permission or approval requests, persisting a pending approval request with task, chat, repo, session, request detail, and Codex-provided option metadata, sending a bounded redacted Telegram approval message with one inline keyboard button per Codex option, mapping exactly three Codex options to exactly three Bot buttons when Codex provides three options, handling button callbacks by resolving only the matching pending request and delivering the selected option back to the running Codex task, preserving existing /approve, /reject, /always_allow, and reply yes/no flows as fallback only for compatible options, supporting a mapped always-reject Codex option and optional /always_reject <request_id> fallback when compatible, rejecting unsafe callback data plus unknown, expired, resolved, unauthorized, or option-incompatible requests, and adding unit, harness, contract, and fake Telegram API coverage without making real Codex or Telegram network calls.

Change /agent task timeout policy so general Codex agent tasks no longer inherit the legacy 10 minute read-only ask timeout by default; add AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_MS startup configuration that parses as a positive integer milliseconds value, rejects invalid configured values during startup, and applies the configured timeout to /agent, /agent new, /agent resume <session_id> <instruction>, and /agent resume --last tasks; keep /continue timeoutMs null and preserve /stop as the user-controlled termination mechanism; preserve task metadata, finalResult extraction, completion pushes, permission approval bridging, shell-disabled spawning, Telegram truncation, and secret redaction; update README and deployment documentation; and add unit, harness, startup, and smoke coverage proving default agent timeoutMs null and configured timeout values are honored.

Fix Codex JSONL session extraction for /agent new by trusting real-shaped thread.started events with thread_id and threadId before assistant output, preserving existing trusted session and conversation metadata support, rejecting assistant prose and command output lookalikes, persisting the discovered thread id as task codexSessionId, replacing any older chat plus repository binding after /agent new completes, making the next plain /agent resume the newly bound thread, preserving /agent resume and /agent resume --last behavior, preserving task id versus Codex session id separation, and adding unit, task executor, harness, and init verification coverage using real-shaped local Codex JSONL output.

Implement explicit agent chat mode scoped by authorized Telegram chatId and selected repository alias so /agent, /agent new, /agent resume <session_id> <instruction>, and /agent resume --last <instruction> enter chat mode, authorized ordinary non-command text in chat mode with a current session binding resumes that session without requiring the /agent prefix, slash commands remain command-first, ordinary text without repo, without chat mode, without a session binding, or while another active agent task is running in the same repository is rejected with clear bounded responses, /agent session reports repo, session, and mode status, /agent exit disables only the current chat plus repository mode flag without deleting task history, /continue is removed from the public command whitelist, help, README, deployment docs, BotFather command menu docs, contract tests, and harness expectations so it returns Unknown command, and unit, harness, contract, smoke, and init coverage verify the behavior while preserving task metadata, finalResult extraction, completion pushes, approval bridging, secret redaction, Telegram truncation, and shell-disabled spawning.

Fix Bot-started Codex exec task startup so /agent, /agent new, /agent resume <session_id> <instruction>, /agent resume --last <instruction>, and ordinary agent chat mode follow-ups do not hang at `Reading additional input from stdin...` because the Bot leaves a piped child stdin open while also passing the prompt as a command argument; default task spawning must use ignored or closed stdin unless an explicitly supported interactive protocol opts in, preserve shell-disabled spawning, task metadata, logs, finalResult extraction, session extraction, completion pushes, timeout handling, /stop, /logs, Telegram truncation, secret redaction, and approval request detection from output, make approval decision writing fail clearly when no writable child stdin exists instead of blocking task startup, and add unit, harness, and init regression coverage proving the non-blocking stdin policy.

Add automated guardrail coverage for the AGENTS.md external behavior verification rule by asserting the rule remains present and includes the core requirements that unknown external behavior must not be inferred from mocks, process semantic changes such as argv, stdio, cwd, env, timeout, signal handling, or shell mode require real command verification or documented inability to verify, structured output fields require real-shaped fixtures, and mocks or fake children only prove repository state-machine behavior; add a real Codex CLI stdin behavior probe that is disabled by default unless explicitly enabled by environment variable, document how to run it, keep default ./init.sh deterministic without Codex CLI, network, Telegram, or OpenAI account requirements, and preserve existing F040 non-blocking stdin unit and harness coverage.

Add a safe Bot-local /approval_test command for authorized Telegram chats that creates a pending test approval request in runtime_state.json without starting Codex, executing shell commands, killing processes, requiring a selected repository, requiring a running task, or depending on writable child stdin; the generated request must be marked as Bot-local test data, include approve, reject, always allow, and always reject options, return or send a bounded response with the generated request id and instructions for /approve <request_id>, /reject <request_id>, /always_allow <request_id>, and /always_reject <request_id>, support inline keyboard callback selection where transports support it, support existing approval command and reply flows, persist resulting statuses and allow rules, avoid taskExecutor.resolveApprovalOption for Bot-local test requests, add /approval_test to the command whitelist, help output, README command surface, BotFather command menu documentation, and contract tests, preserve real approval request behavior and F040 non-blocking stdin policy, and add unit, harness, contract, smoke, and init coverage.

Fix Codex session metadata extraction so /agent new tasks still bind the discovered Codex thread when real-shaped logs contain harmless Codex CLI prelude lines such as stdinMode=ignore and Reading additional input from stdin... before top-level JSONL thread.started metadata; trust top-level structured Codex session metadata events before assistant or user-facing output starts, preserve rejection of assistant prose, item.completed agent messages, command execution output, source/test/docs output, and log-tail session lookalikes, persist the discovered thread id to both task codexSessionId and askSessions chat-plus-repo binding, make the next ordinary agent chat mode follow-up resume that newly bound thread instead of returning the no-session-bound error, preserve task id versus Codex session id separation, F039 chat mode behavior, F040 non-blocking stdin policy, F041 real-shaped fixture expectations, and F042 approval-test behavior, and add unit, task executor, harness, and init regression coverage using the observed local Codex JSONL log shape.

Add a safe /camera_clip <seconds> command for authorized Telegram chats that records a short local camera clip and sends it as Telegram video only when ENABLE_CAMERA_CLIP_COMMAND=1 and CAMERA_CLIP_COMMAND_JSON provides a shell-disabled argv template with {seconds} and {output}; validate seconds as an integer from 1 through 10; reject unauthorized chats, disabled command, invalid duration, missing or malformed capture config, concurrent capture, capture timeout, missing or empty output, and send failures with bounded responses; never start Codex, never live stream, never run through shell, never require selected repository, never keep recordings after send or failure, delete temp files, avoid logging media content, support Telegram sendVideo in polling and webhook transports, update command whitelist, help, README, deployment, BotFather docs, contract tests, and add unit, harness, contract, smoke, and init coverage using fake capture and fake Telegram transport without real camera or network requirements.

Remove the temporary home-watch /camera_clip capability from agent-remote-tg now that it lives in /Users/armstrong/Project/home-watch-tg; remove the command from whitelist, parser requirements, help, README, deployment docs, BotFather docs, app handler, startup config parsing, Telegram sendVideo handling, camera capture source, and camera-only tests; keep historical F044 records unchanged, preserve all non-camera /agent, agent chat mode, repository, polling, webhook, approval, /approval_test, task management, completion push, and text Telegram behavior; document that home-watch camera functionality moved to home-watch-tg; verify /camera_clip returns Unknown command, no source imports camera-clip.js, no src/camera-clip.js remains, default tests do not reference camera clip or sendVideo, and ./init.sh passes.

Add a Bot-local /git_commit_push <message> command that lets authorized Telegram chats commit and push changes in the currently selected /use repository without going through Codex CLI; require a selected whitelisted repo and non-empty commit message; preview current branch, git status --short, and staged files before mutation; create a pending approval request tied to repo alias, cwd, branch, explicit file list, and commit message; require /approve or inline approval before running fixed-argv shell-disabled git add -- <explicit paths>, git commit -m <message>, and git push origin <current-branch>; reject approval leaves the repo untouched; prevent arbitrary shell, user-provided git flags, unwhitelisted paths, traversal, and operation outside the selected repo; report commit failures separately from push failures; classify recognizable network/DNS and authentication/permission push failures; update command whitelist, help, README, deployment docs, BotFather docs, and add unit, harness, contract, smoke, and init coverage while preserving existing /agent, agent chat mode, repository, approval, /approval_test, polling, webhook, and task management behavior.

Relax the /agent Codex prompt so repository agents can use their available local tools for investigation, implementation, and verification instead of being told to keep shell execution disabled; preserve Bot-owned shell=false and fixed-argv process safety, Telegram authorization, /git_commit_push approval constraints, repository files and git history as the source of truth, no reliance on Telegram chat history, AGENTS.md requirements for implementation requests, active sandbox and approval-policy respect, and action/change/verification/remaining-issue summaries; update docs if they describe the old prompt behavior; add unit, contract, harness, smoke, and init coverage proving the prompt no longer contains the broad shell-disabled prohibition while still preserving the required safety and repository workflow language.
